title: Condemning the wave of domestic terrorism attacks targeting Tesla cars and dealerships.
plain_summary: This House resolution says that attacks on Tesla cars and car dealerships are domestic terrorism and should be condemned. The resolution lists specific cases where people damaged, burned, or attacked Tesla properties in different states in early 2025. Congress is officially saying these attacks are wrong and dangerous. The resolution treats these incidents as serious acts of terrorism rather than just regular crimes.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Title
sections.text: This resolution condemns the wave of domestic terrorism attacks targeting Tesla cars and dealerships.

sections.title: Sponsors
sections.text: On April first, two thousand twenty-five, Representative Boebert introduced this resolution along with nine other House members. The resolution was sent to the House Judiciary Committee.

sections.title: Findings
sections.text: The House finds that members of the Democratic Party have called for their supporters to attack Tesla vehicles and buildings to protest Elon Musk. Anti-Tesla incidents have happened in at least nine states and have included arson (deliberately setting fires), gunfire, and vandalism (damaging property). At least eighty reported cases of vandalism or arson of Tesla vehicles have happened across the United States and Canada. At least ten Tesla dealerships, charging stations, and facilities have been attacked by vandals. On February eleventh, two thousand twenty-five, in Loveland, Colorado, a man attacked a Tesla dealer multiple times by throwing Molotov cocktails (homemade firebombs) and spray-painting words including Nazi on vehicles and windows. On March second, two thousand twenty-five, in Washington DC, two people wrote political hate speech on Tesla vehicles twice before running away. On March third, two thousand twenty-five, in Boston, Massachusetts, seven Tesla charging stations at a mall were set on fire. On March sixth, two thousand twenty-five, in Portland, Oregon, at least seven gunshots were fired into a Tesla showroom at night, windows were broken, three cars were damaged, and one bullet went through an office wall into a computer monitor. On March seventh, two thousand twenty-five, in North Charleston, South Carolina, a twenty-four-year-old man threw five Molotov cocktails at a Tesla charging station, and witnesses saw a man spray-paint a curse word against Trump and Long Live Ukraine in red paint in the parking lot. On March seventh, two thousand twenty-five, in Loveland, Colorado, a man lit an explosive device and threw it at a Tesla dealership, where it landed between two cars and started a fire that put lives at risk, and rocks were also thrown that damaged the building and multiple vehicles. On March eighteenth, two thousand twenty-five, in Las Vegas, Nevada, a person dressed in black shot at Tesla cars at a repair shop and set two of them on fire with Molotov cocktails, and the word Resist was spray-painted on the front doors. On March twenty-fourth, two thousand twenty-five, Austin police found multiple explosive devices that had been placed at a Tesla showroom in Austin, Texas. On March twenty-fourth, two thousand twenty-five, the Federal Bureau of Investigation created a special team to work with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives to investigate violent attacks on Tesla properties.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives condemns the wave of domestic terrorism attacks targeting Tesla cars and dealerships.
